message.content: check the pr to see if Copilot has left any comments.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
Copilot left **one review** on PR #38, with **no inline comments** but one suppressed low-confidence note worth addressing:

**Concern (ContactManagerApp.swift:132):** In `CONTACTMANAGER_UI_TEST_MODE` the app still builds a CloudKit-backed container (`cloudKitDatabase: .automatic`). Combined with the forced seeding, a developer running UI tests while signed into iCloud could push the sample contacts to their CloudKit DB and sync them across devices. Copilot suggests forcing a local-only configuration (CloudKit disabled) in UI-test mode.

That's a legitimate point — the seed-on-launch behavior makes the CloudKit risk real. Want me to fix it (force `cloudKitDatabase: .none` when `CONTACTMANAGER_UI_TEST_MODE` is set)?

content: You asked me to check PR #38 (XCUITest smoke tests) for Copilot comments. Copilot flagged one issue: UI-test mode still uses CloudKit, risking sample contacts syncing to iCloud. Next: decide whether to force local-only container in UI-test mode. (disable recaps in /config)

message.content: Yes lets fix it... tests shouldn't use cloudkit and always mock out calls that might affect local state of the real application. So you could use in-memorty database or a different fil

lastPrompt: Yes lets fix it... tests shouldn't use cloudkit and always mock out calls that might affect local state of the real application. So you could use in-memorty database or a different fil

content.text: I'll switch UI-test mode to an in-memory, CloudKit-disabled configuration. That removes both risks: no iCloud sync, and no on-disk file that could leak between runs or collide with the user's real store.

content.text: Let me verify the full edited file looks right.

content.content:
96	    // MARK: - Container loading
97	
98	    private static func loadContainer(resettingStore: Bool = false) -> ContainerLoadState {
99	        // UI test mode is detected via env var and pre-empts the unit-test
100	        // guard below: XCUITest injects an automation-support library
101	        // that drags `XCTestCase` into the app's loaded classes, so the
102	        // `NSClassFromString` check would short-circuit to `.testing` and
103	        // render `EmptyView()`. UI tests run against an in-memory,
104	        // CloudKit-disabled container so they never touch the user's real
105	        // on-disk store or push sample contacts into iCloud.
106	        let isUITestMode = ProcessInfo.processInfo
107	            .environment["CONTACTMANAGER_UI_TEST_MODE"] != nil
108	
109	        if !isUITestMode {
110	            // When hosting the unit tests, skip building the app's model
111	            // container entirely so the test target owns the only
112	            // container in the process.
113	            let isUnitTesting = ProcessInfo.processInfo
114	                .environment["XCTestConfigurationFilePath"] != nil
115	                || NSClassFromString("XCTestCase") != nil
116	            if isUnitTesting { return .testing }
117	        }
118	
119	        if resettingStore {
120	            deleteDefaultStore()
121	        }
122	
123	        let schema = Schema([Contact.self, ContactField.self, ContactGroup.self])
124	
125	        // UI-test mode uses an in-memory, CloudKit-disabled container so
126	        // tests are deterministic and can't sync sample contacts to a
127	        // signed-in iCloud account or pollute the user's local store.
128	        if isUITestMode {
129	            do {
130	                let configuration = ModelConfiguration(
131	                    schema: schema,
132	                    isStoredInMemoryOnly: true,
133	                    cloudKitDatabase: .none
134	                )
135	                let container = try ModelContainer(for: schema, configurations: configuration)
136	                do {
137	                    try SampleData.seedIfNeeded(container.mainContext)
138	                } catch {
139	                    print("ContactManager: sample data seeding skipped — \(error)")
140	                }
141	                EntityModelContainer.shared = container
142	                return .ready(container)
143	            } catch {
144	                return .failed(error.localizedDescription)
145	            }
146	        }
147	
148	        // Build a CloudKit-backed container when the iCloud capability is
149	        // present, and fall back to a local-only container otherwise so the
150	        // app still runs without iCloud.
151	        let container: ModelContainer
152	        var isLocalOnlyFallback = false
153	        do {
154	            // `.automatic` uses the project's iCloud container when an iCloud
155	            // entitlement is present; without one it still loads successfully
156	            // and behaves as a local-only store.
157	            let cloudConfiguration = ModelConfiguration(schema: schema, cloudKitDatabase: .automatic)
158	            container = try ModelContainer(for: schema, configurations: cloudConfiguration)
159	        } catch {
160	            print("ContactManager: CloudKit container failed (\(error.localizedDescription)); using local.")
161	            do {
162	                let localConfiguration = ModelConfiguration(schema: schema, cloudKitDatabase: .none)
163	                container = try ModelContainer(for: schema, configurations: localConfiguration)
164	                isLocalOnlyFallback = true
165	            } catch {
166	                return .failed(error.localizedDescription)
167	            }
168	        }
169	
170	        // Only seed sample data on the confirmed local-only fallback. On a
171	        // CloudKit-capable container the user's real contacts might be about
172	        // to sync down (a fresh install on a second device); seeding then
173	        // would race the sync and propagate the samples to every device.
174	        if isLocalOnlyFallback {
175	            do {
176	                try SampleData.seedIfNeeded(container.mainContext)
177	            } catch {
178	                print("ContactManager: sample data seeding skipped — \(error)")
179	            }
180	        }
181	        // Publish the container for App Intents (Shortcuts/Spotlight queries)
182	        // and kick off an initial Spotlight reindex.
183	        EntityModelContainer.shared = container
184	        Task.detached { await SpotlightIndexer.shared.reindex() }
185	        return .ready(container)

content.text: Those SourceKit diagnostics are spurious — they're "cannot find in scope" for types defined elsewhere in the same module, which means SourceKit doesn't have the module context loaded. Let me verify with a real build.

input.command:
git add ContactManager/App/ContactManagerApp.swift && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
UI test mode: in-memory, CloudKit-disabled container

Addresses Copilot review on #38: UI-test mode previously kept
`cloudKitDatabase: .automatic`, so a developer running UI tests while
signed into iCloud could push the seeded sample contacts to their
CloudKit database. Switch UI tests to `isStoredInMemoryOnly: true` +
`cloudKitDatabase: .none` so they never touch the on-disk store or
sync to iCloud.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git status
